articles.title: Here are the 30,000 songs Sony is suing Udio’s AI music generator over
articles.summary: Sony Music Entertainment has filed another lawsuit against Udio, accusing the AI music generator of infringing the copyright of more than 30,000 of its songs, ranging from Elvis Presley's Hound Dog to Beyoncé's Say My Name, and Harry Styles' As It Was. The lawsuit, filed in a New York court on Monday, claims that this […]

articles.title: Gritt exits stealth with $34 million for robots to build solar plants—then, everything else
articles.summary: Gritt is coming out of stealth with $34 million and plan to automate the hardest tasks on construction sites.
articles.full_body:
Title: Gritt exits stealth with $34 million for robots to build solar plants—then, everything else

URL Source: https://techcrunch.com/2026/07/21/gritt-exits-stealth-with-34-million-for-robots-to-build-solar-plants-then-everything-else

Published Time: 2026-07-21T10:00:00+00:00

Markdown Content:
One of the most important things happening on Earth today is the solar energy build-out. Around the world, companies and countries are racing to deploy solar and batteries to achieve energy independence and limit the effects of climate change.

That build-out, though, is running into a labor market challenge, with a limited supply of workers to meet a growing demand for installation. Robots could be an answer, but industrial robots have historically struggled in unstructured environments, at least until now. The latest generation of AI models may have changed that equation.

That’s the driving idea behind [Gritt](http://gritt.ai/), a start-up founded by two Carnegie Mellon-trained roboticists, CEO Puneet Puri and CTO Vishal Dugar. The company exited stealth Tuesday morning with a $26 million Series A round of funding led by Obvious Ventures with participation from Union Square Ventures and Active Impact Investment. That brings its total funding to $34 million, following an earlier seed round backed by First Round Capital, Climactic, Congruent Ventures, and VSC Ventures. The startup is building an intelligent system to “help civilization build infrastructure faster,” in Puri’s words.

“Our thesis is that if we truly want to speed up construction,” Puri tells TechCrunch, “you need an intelligence which can work in the outdoor, chaotic environments of these construction sites, and it has to be generalizable enough that it can work in these varied environments.”

Rather than building its own robots from scratch, Gritt uses off-the-shelf hardware—thus far, rented skidders and robotic arms built by companies like Kawasaki—to build platforms that are controlled by its AI models. The first job its systems handle is unloading large, glass solar panels, carrying them toward the metal frames where they need to be installed, and positioning them on the frames with sub-millimeter accuracy so workers can fasten them.

“There are people who used to build rockets that went into space and had infinite budget for the smallest little part, and then there are people who know what it means to get into dirty, dull, and dangerous jobs and scale them like mad,” said Andrew Beebe, the partner at Obvious Ventures who led Gritt’s Series A round. “These guys are in the second camp, and that’s a special kind of entrepreneur that has the technical chops, the AI, and the machine vision skills to make it work.”

Gritt has two systems currently deployed in the field, using the data they collect to improve their behavior. Puri says that a typical eight-person crew workers can install 800 panels a day, but the same crew working with Gritt’s systems can install 3,000 to 4,000 panels each day.

Now, the company says it is contracted to help install 2.8 gigawatts of solar panels in the next 18 months, and that its customers include three of the top 10 US power construction companies. The company hopes to be operating 48 of its systems within the next six months.

TechCrunch spoke to one Gritt customer who declined to be identified for competitive reasons, but who was enthusiastic about the system’s ability to improve his work. He expects it to be easier to work at remote sites where it is difficult to attract workers, and anticipates a reduction in injuries since workers won’t have to repeatedly lift 100-pound panels overhead.

Gritt is competing against companies with their own panel-installing robots like [Luminous Robotics](https://www.luminousrobotics.com/), [Cosmic](https://www.cosmicrobotics.com/), and China’s [Trinabot](https://www.trinarobot.com/solution/). Those companies are building their own hardware, rather than focusing on off-the-shelf vehicles and arms like Gritt, a difference that could shape who grows faster and with a leaner cost structure as demand grows.

Gritt wants to add new manipulation tasks to its system so it can fasten the solar panels, drill posts, and even build the racks they sit on. Longer term, it also wants to move into other common, labor-intensive construction tasks, like tying rebar before concrete is poured over it.

What’s enabled the startup to pursue this vision? Mainly, the rise of new AI models, the founders say.

“Making a system for one solution was still possible to some extent five years ago, right?” Puri said, but AI is now making that work generalizable — the same underlying pipeline can be reused and improve across tasks. As an example, he noted that training the system to stack cinder blocks took weeks, while a similar demo with rebar tying took just a day using the same software.

But training new tasks is just the beginning of Gritt’s vision. The founders believe the suite of sensors and intelligence its systems bring to worksites can do more than install panels; it can boost management and decision-making. For instance, they imagine their system noticing a trench is open while a storm approaches, allowing it to alert workers to cover it before rain damages components, or flagging missing inventory.

“Gritt becomes now this layer of physical AI, which is doing this dextrous, labor-intensive task, plus it can help you take decisions on the site,” Puri said.

_When you purchase through links in our articles, [we may earn a small commission](https://techcrunch.com/techcrunch-affiliate-monetization-standards/). This doesn’t affect our editorial independence._

Tim Fernholz is a journalist who writes about technology, finance and public policy. He has closely covered the rise of the private space industry and is the author of _Rocket Billionaires: Elon Musk, Jeff Bezos and the New Space Race._ Formerly, he was a senior reporter at Quartz, the global business news site, for more than a decade, and began his career as a political reporter in Washington, D.C. You can contact or verify outreach from Tim by emailing tim.fernholz@techcrunch.com or via an encrypted message to tim_fernholz.21 on Signal.

[View Bio](https://techcrunch.com/author/tim-fernholz/)

articles.title: SpaceX in your index fund, explained
articles.summary: Index funds are touted as one of the safest ways to invest. Rather than picking and choosing individual stocks, index funds let you bet on the market as a whole. articles.title: Adobe’s ‘natural look’ camera app embraces generative AI
articles.summary: Adobe's experimental camera app has taken an unexpected turn. # Adobe’s ‘natural look’ camera app embraces generative AI

Project Indigo’s new AI Playground update introduces generative filter effects, object removal, and chatbot feedback.

Project Indigo’s new AI Playground update introduces generative filter effects, object removal, and chatbot feedback.

by Jess Weatherbed

![Image 3: Jess Weatherbed](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/chorus/author_profile_images/195820/JESSICA_

articles.title: WAIC重磅成果｜上海仪电智算牵头成立“智算系统架构联盟”并发布《超节点系统架构规范》
articles.full_body:
Title: WAIC重磅成果｜上海仪电智算牵头成立“智算系统架构联盟”并发布《超节点系统架构规范》

URL Source: https://www.qbitai.com/2026/07/456052.html

Markdown Content:
< img id="wx_img" src="https://www.qbitai.com/wp-content/uploads/imgs/qbitai-logo-1.png" width="400" height="400">

2026-07-21 17:39:34 来源：[量子位](https://www.qbitai.com/)

在上海市经济和信息化委员会指导下，由上海仪电智算牵头，联合GPU芯片、互连、整机、大模型、软件等20余家产业链上下游主体共同发起的“智算系统架构联盟”（以下简称“联盟”）在2026世界人工智能大会上正式成立，同步发布《超节点系统架构规范》（以下简称“规范”）。联盟立足上海、辐射全国，旨在汇聚生态合力、共筑开放架构，为智算基础设施高质量建设保驾护航，并明确由仪电—中兴联合实验室负责日常运营。

**锚定智算底座建设，破解产业升级核心痛点**

智能算力已成为数字经济发展的核心驱动力，但算力资源供需不平衡、软硬件适配壁垒高等问题制约着产业整体升级。上海仪电作为上海市智能算力公共服务平台的核心企业，已建成数座智算中心，累计完成数十款国产AI核心组件的测试验证，形成百余份测试报告，并自主研发“仪电智算云平台”，实现异构算力的统一纳管与智能调度。联盟以“聚全栈生态筑智算系统架构，践国企担当创自主可控底座”为核心使命，面向智算中心、超算集群、行业算力底座等场景，提供系统化、一体化的规划与建设方案。

**构建多元组织架构，打造全栈生态协作平台**

![Image 1](https://i.qbitai.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/b075c372dde79b6dfd80f441a5de66de.jpeg)

联盟由仪电智算牵头，联合阶跃星辰、稀宇科技、天数智芯、壁仞科技、平头哥、寒武纪、东方算芯、沐曦股份、燧原科技、思朗科技、中兴通讯、新华三、浪潮集团、算丰信息、曙光信息、曦智科技、驭驯网络、清程极智、趋境科技、基流科技、硅基流动、云脉芯联、云合智网等23家智算产业链上下游企业共同发起（排名不分先后），从底层芯片到上层应用的完整产业链协作体系，聚合异构算力与技术资源，打破算力壁垒，推动软硬件协同适配。联盟成员可深度参与超节点建设规范等行业标准的研讨与编制，优先使用联盟真机测试验证环境，高效完成产品适配与性能调优，缩短研发迭代周期；同时依托联盟平台联动龙头企业与国家级实验室，开展前沿技术协同攻关，联合申报重大科研专项与示范工程，打通产业链上下游合作渠道，推动供需精准匹配与业务协同。

**发布联盟首项规范，确立智算行业技术统一基准**

《超节点系统架构规范》（1.0版）作为联盟首项成果，分为硬件与软件两部分。硬件方面，采用冷板式液冷整机柜，统一节点尺寸并通过电、液、网三总线盲插实现前维护，配备集中供电、液冷散热与漏液联动隔离，通过高速互连背板实现Scale-up部署；软件方面，规范了运维管理、集群纳管与拓扑感知调度、集合通信、存储与数据加载、安全可信及符合性验收等要求。后续，上海仪电将据此规范打造自主超节点集群。

![Image 2](https://i.qbitai.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/93a8dfeb4fac5a50aef7fdf3274ea8b9.jpeg)

联盟成立与规范发布，标志着上海在构建自主可控智算产业生态上迈出坚实一步。未来，联盟将持续拓展合作网络，以系统化规划方案与全栈生态协同合力，成为智算基础设施建设的重要加速器，为上海加快建设人工智能高地贡献力量。

_本文由仪电智算提供，量子位获授权转载，观点归原作者所有。_

_版权所有，未经授权不得以任何形式转载及使用，违者必究。_

fact_packs.headline: Gritt exits stealth with $34 million for robots to build solar plants—then, everything else
facts: Title: Gritt exits stealth with $34 million for robots to build solar plants—then, everything else

One of the most important things happening on Earth today is the solar energy build-out. Around the world, companies and countries are racing to deploy solar and batteries to achieve energy independence and limit the effects of climate change.

That build-out, though, is running into a labor market challenge, with a limited supply of workers to meet a growing demand for installation. Robots could be an answer, but industrial robots have historically struggled in unstructured environments, at least until now. The latest generation of AI models may have changed that equation.

That’s the driving idea behind [Gritt](http://gritt.ai/), a start-up founded by two Carnegie Mellon-trained roboticists, CEO Puneet Puri and CTO Vishal Dugar. The company exited stealth Tuesday morning with a $26 million Series A round of funding led by Obvious Ventures with participation from Union Square Ventures and Active Impact Investment. That brings its total funding to $34 million, following an earlier seed round backed by First Round Capital, Climactic, Congruent Ventures, and VSC Ventures. The startup is building an intelligent system to “help civilization build infrastructure faster,” in Puri’s words.

“Our thesis is that if we truly want to speed up construction,” Puri tells TechCrunch, “you need an intelligence which can work in the outdoor, chaotic environments of these construction sites, and it has to be generalizable enough that it can work in these varied environments.”

Rather than building its own robots from scratch, Gritt uses off-the-shelf hardware—thus far, rented skidders and robotic arms built by companies like Kawasaki—to build platforms that are controlled by its AI models. The first job its systems handle is unloading large, glass solar panels, carrying them toward the metal frames where they need to be installed, and positioning them on the frames with sub-millimeter accuracy so workers can fasten them.

“There are people who used to build rockets that went into space and had infinite budget for the smallest little part, and then there are people who know what it means to get into dirty, dull, and dangerous jobs and scale them like mad,” said Andrew Beebe, the partner at Obvious Ventures who led Gritt’s Series A round. “These guys are in the second camp, and that’s a special kind of entrepreneur that has the technical chops, the AI, and the machine vision skills to make it work.”

Gritt has two systems currently deployed in the field, using the data they collect to improve their behavior. Puri says that a typical eight-person crew workers can install 800 panels a day, but the same crew working with Gritt’s systems can install 3,000 to 4,000 panels each day.

sources.title: Gritt exits stealth with $34 million for robots to build solar plants—then, everything else

reading.title: 伊恩每日·科技：AI版权诉讼、太阳能机器人、SpaceX入指数、Adobe AI相机、智算架构联盟
reading.lead: 今日科技焦点：索尼对AI音乐生成器Udio发起大规模版权诉讼，列出3万首侵权歌曲；机器人初创Gritt获3400万美元融资，用于太阳能电站建设；SpaceX即将纳入纳斯达克100指数，影响指数基金投资者；Adobe Indigo相机App新增AI批评功能；上海仪电牵头成立智算系统架构联盟，发布超节点规范。

sections.title: 索尼诉Udio：3万首歌曲的AI版权边界
sections.dek: 索尼音乐娱乐对AI音乐生成平台Udio提起的版权诉讼中，列出了超过3万首被指控侵权的歌曲。
sections.body: 2026年7月20日，索尼音乐娱乐在针对AI音乐生成平台Udio的版权诉讼中，提交了一份包含超过3万首歌曲的清单。这些歌曲涵盖了索尼旗下艺人的大量作品。诉讼的核心在于，Udio的AI模型被指控未经授权使用这些受版权保护的歌曲进行训练，并生成与之相似的音乐。此前，唱片公司已对多个AI音乐工具发起法律挑战，但索尼此次列出的歌曲数量创下纪录。从技术角度看，AI音乐生成模型通常需要大量音乐数据进行训练，而版权方认为这种使用方式不属于合理使用。产业层面，此案可能为AI训练数据的版权合规性设定重要先例。如果索尼胜诉，AI音乐公司可能需要与版权方达成授权协议，增加运营成本；若Udio胜诉，则可能鼓励更多AI公司直接抓取数据。对普通人而言，这意味着未来用AI生成音乐时，可能面临更严格的版权限制，或需支付额外费用。后续观察重点在于法院是否认定AI训练属于侵权，以及双方是否会达成和解。
sections.takeaway: AI音乐生成面临版权红线，3万首歌曲诉讼或重塑行业规则。

source_refs.title: Here are the 30,000 songs Sony is suing Udio’s AI music generator over
Sony Names 30,000 Songs in Expanded Udio AI Copyright Lawsuit - The Tech Buzz

sections.title: Gritt获3400万美元：机器人建造太阳能电站
sections.dek: 机器人初创公司Gritt完成3400万美元融资，用AI控制的机器人安装太阳能板，效率提升数倍。
sections.body: 2026年7月21日，机器人初创公司Gritt宣布获得3400万美元融资，其中Series A轮2600万美元由Obvious Ventures领投，Union Square Ventures等跟投。Gritt由两位卡内基梅隆大学机器人学背景的创始人领导，其核心产品是使用现成硬件（如滑移装载机和机械臂）结合自研AI模型，在户外复杂环境中自动搬运和安装太阳能板。据公司称，一个8人团队使用其系统每天可安装3000至4000块面板，而传统人工方法只能安装约800块。这一效率提升源于AI对非结构化环境的适应能力，解决了太阳能建设劳动力短缺的痛点。从产业角度看，太阳能装机量全球激增，但安装工人供不应求，机器人自动化成为关键解决方案。Gritt的策略是先用太阳能安装切入市场，未来拓展到其他基础设施建设。对普通人而言，这意味着太阳能电站建设成本可能下降，推动清洁能源普及，但也可能减少部分安装岗位。后续观察：Gritt能否在更多工地规模化部署，以及竞争对手的跟进速度。
sections.takeaway: AI机器人将太阳能安装效率提升4倍，清洁能源建设加速。

source_refs.title: Gritt exits stealth with $34 million for robots to build solar plants—then, everything else

sections.title: SpaceX纳入纳斯达克100：指数基金投资者的新变量
sections.dek: SpaceX将于2026年7月7日加入纳斯达克100指数，这意味着数百万指数基金投资者将间接持有这家太空公司。
sections.body: 据多家媒体报道，SpaceX将于2026年7月7日正式被纳入纳斯达克100指数。该指数由纳斯达克交易所市值最大的100家非金融公司组成，是许多指数基金（如QQQ）的跟踪标的。这意味着，任何持有跟踪纳斯达克100指数的基金（如ETF或共同基金）的投资者，都将自动获得SpaceX的敞口。SpaceX目前是估值最高的私营公司之一，其业务涵盖火箭发射、星链卫星互联网和星舰项目。纳入指数后，指数基金将被动买入SpaceX股票，可能推高其股价，并增加其在指数中的权重。对普通投资者而言，这是一个间接投资SpaceX的机会，无需直接购买其股票（目前仍为私营）。但需注意，指数基金的投资决策由指数规则驱动，而非主动选择，因此投资者无法控制对SpaceX的配置比例。此外，SpaceX的高估值和波动性可能影响指数表现。建议投资者审视自己持有的指数基金，了解其成分股变化，并评估是否与自身风险偏好匹配。
sections.takeaway: SpaceX入指数，散户可通过基金间接投资太空经济，但需注意被动配置风险。

source_refs.title: SpaceX in your index fund, explained
Buckle Up, S&P 500 and Nasdaq Index Fund Investors. SpaceX Could Soon Become 1 of Your Largest Positions. - Yahoo Finance

sections.title: Adobe Indigo相机App：AI批评你的照片
sections.dek: Adobe的Indigo相机App新增AI功能，能对用户拍摄的照片提供批评和改进建议，强调“自然外观”。
sections.body: 2026年7月20日，Adobe宣布其相机应用Indigo进行重大更新，新增生成式AI功能，包括一个名为“AI批评”的特性。该功能可以分析用户拍摄的照片，从构图、曝光、色彩等方面给出反馈，并建议如何改进。Indigo最初定位为“自然外观”相机，强调减少过度处理，但此次更新加入了AI增强编辑能力。从技术上看，这利用了Adobe在图像识别和生成式AI领域的积累，模型经过大量专业摄影作品训练。与市面上其他AI修图工具不同，Indigo的AI批评功能更侧重于教育用户，而非一键美化。产业层面，这表明AI正在从“替代摄影师”转向“辅助摄影师”，降低专业摄影的门槛。对普通人而言，这意味着即使没有摄影知识，也能通过AI反馈逐步提升拍摄水平，但同时也可能让照片风格趋于同质化。后续观察：该功能是否会扩展到Adobe其他产品，以及用户对AI批评的接受度。
sections.takeaway: Adobe用AI教用户拍照，从自动修图转向摄影教育。

source_refs.title: Adobe’s ‘natural look’ camera app embraces generative AI
Adobe camera app’s new feature will critique your photos using AI

sections.title: 上海仪电牵头成立智算系统架构联盟，发布超节点规范
sections.dek: 上海仪电智算联合23家企业成立联盟，发布《超节点系统架构规范》，旨在解决算力供需不平衡和软硬件适配问题。
sections.body: 2026年7月21日，在上海世界人工智能大会（WAIC）上，由上海仪电智算牵头，联合阶跃星辰、天数智芯、壁仞科技、平头哥、寒武纪、中兴通讯、新华三等23家产业链企业，共同成立了“智算系统架构联盟”，并发布《超节点系统架构规范》。该联盟旨在汇聚芯片、互连、整机、大模型、软件等环节的生态力量，构建开放统一的智算系统架构。背景是智能算力需求激增，但存在算力资源供需不平衡、不同厂商硬件软件适配困难等痛点。上海仪电作为上海智能算力公共服务平台核心企业，已建成多座智算中心，并自主研发了异构算力统一纳管平台。规范发布后，联盟成员可参与标准制定，使用真机测试环境，缩短产品适配周期。从产业角度看，这类似于建立智算领域的“安卓生态”，通过统一标准降低产业链协作成本。对普通人而言，这意味着未来AI服务可能更稳定、成本更低，但也可能增强头部企业的议价能力。后续观察：联盟能否吸引更多国际企业加入，以及规范是否成为行业标准。
sections.takeaway: 上海成立智算联盟，统一架构标准，降低AI基础设施成本。

source_refs.title: WAIC重磅成果｜上海仪电智算牵头成立“智算系统架构联盟”并发布《超节点系统架构规范》

reading.synthesis: 今日五条新闻共同指向一个趋势：AI正从软件渗透到硬件、能源、金融和基础设施。索尼诉Udio标志着版权博弈进入量化阶段；Gritt的机器人展示了AI在物理世界的生产力提升；SpaceX入指数让太空经济进入普通人的投资组合；Adobe的AI批评功能则把专业能力平民化；而上海智算联盟试图从底层架构统一算力生态。这些变化的核心驱动力都是AI，但落地路径各异——法律、机器人、金融、消费软件、基础设施。对普通人来说，机会在于更低成本的能源和服务，风险在于版权、就业和投资波动。建议关注AI在具体行业的落地节奏，而非泛泛的炒作。

podcast.title: 伊恩每日·科技：AI音乐版权大战、太阳能机器人、SpaceX进指数、Adobe AI相机、智算架构联盟
podcast.description: 本期节目从Sony起诉Udio侵犯3万首歌曲版权切入，探讨AI音乐合法性的边界；接着讲述Gritt用34万美元融资让机器人建造太阳能电站，提高效率4倍；然后解释SpaceX加入纳斯达克100对指数基金投资者的影响；再聊聊Adobe Indigo相机App如何用生成式AI改变摄影；最后聚焦上海WAIC上成立的智算系统架构联盟，解读国产算力生态的协作趋势。

blocks.speaker: ian
blocks.text: 今天我想从一首歌开始。想象你写了一首歌，旋律和歌词都是你原创的，然后有一天，你发现一个AI音乐生成器，只要输入几个词就能生成一首风格完全一样的曲子——而且它的训练数据里，很可能就包含了你的歌。这不是假设，这是Sony音乐刚刚对AI音乐公司Udio提起的诉讼里描述的场景。Sony列出了整整30,000首被侵权的歌曲。这期节目，我们就从这场官司出发，看看AI音乐、机器人建电站、SpaceX进指数、Adobe的AI相机，以及上海WAIC上成立的智算架构联盟，这些事件背后共同的逻辑：技术正在重新划定边界，而谁掌握标准，谁就掌握未来。

blocks.speaker: ian
blocks.text: 先看这场诉讼的细节。根据The Verge的报道，Sony在2026年7月20日向Udio提起了扩大后的诉讼，列出了30,000首被侵权的歌曲。这不是一个象征性的数字——Sony是认真的。Udio是一个AI音乐生成平台，用户输入提示词就能生成音乐。Sony声称Udio在未经授权的情况下，用其版权歌曲训练了AI模型。这起案件的关键在于：AI训练使用版权材料是否构成侵权？目前法律还没有明确答案。但Sony的做法表明，音乐行业正在从观望转向主动出击。如果Sony胜诉，可能会迫使AI音乐公司要么支付巨额授权费，要么彻底改变训练方式。

blocks.speaker: listener
blocks.text: 30,000首歌，Sony是怎么证明这些歌被用来训练AI的？难道AI公司会公开他们的训练数据吗？

blocks.speaker: ian
blocks.text: 好问题。Sony的律师团队很可能会通过技术手段来证明：比如让AI生成与这30,000首歌高度相似的片段，或者分析模型输出的特征，反向推断训练数据中包含了这些歌曲。不过，公开报道中没有提到具体证据。这恰恰是这类案件的难点——训练数据通常是黑箱。但Sony选择列出具体歌曲清单，说明他们有相当把握。

blocks.speaker: ian
blocks.text: 从AI音乐版权，我们转到另一个AI应用场景——建造太阳能电站。一家叫Gritt的初创公司刚刚结束隐身模式，宣布获得3400万美元融资，其中2600万美元是A轮，由Obvious Ventures领投，Union Square Ventures等跟投。Gritt的创始人Puneet Puri和Vishal Dugar都是卡内基梅隆大学出身的机器人专家。他们的想法很简单：全球太阳能建设面临劳动力短缺，而AI赋能的机器人可以填补这个缺口。Gritt没有自己造机器人，而是用现成的工程车辆和机械臂，加上他们的AI模型来控制。目前部署的两套系统，可以让一个8人团队每天的安装量从800块太阳能板提升到3000到4000块，效率提高了4到5倍。注意，他们瞄准的不只是太阳能——Puri说目标是“帮助文明更快地建设基础设施”。这意味着未来可能扩展到其他建筑场景。

blocks.speaker: listener
blocks.text: 用现成的硬件加AI控制，听起来门槛不高。那Gritt的核心竞争力是什么？其他公司不能复制吗？

blocks.speaker: ian
blocks.text: 核心竞争力在于AI模型在真实工地环境中的泛化能力。Puri提到，他们的系统要在“户外、混乱的工地环境”中工作，并且要足够通用，能适应不同场地。这种能力来自大量的实地数据训练和算法优化。硬件可以买，但让AI在尘土飞扬、光照变化的工地上保持亚毫米级精度，这需要积累。另外，他们已经部署了两套系统在运行，数据飞轮效应会让他们越来越强。

blocks.speaker: ian
blocks.text: 接下来是一个让普通投资者兴奋的消息：SpaceX即将进入你的指数基金。根据The Verge的报道，SpaceX已被纳入纳斯达克100指数，生效日期是2026年7月7日。这意味着，如果你持有追踪纳斯达克100的指数基金，比如QQQ，你就会间接持有SpaceX的股份。SpaceX目前是私营公司，估值约1500亿美元，但通过指数纳入，散户投资者可以不用直接买股票就能分享其增长。不过，这里有一个重要细节：指数基金持有的是SpaceX的公开交易证券，可能是通过特殊目的收购工具或直接上市后的股票。但无论如何，这标志着太空经济正式进入主流投资组合。

blocks.speaker: ian
blocks.text: 说回AI应用，Adobe旗下的Indigo相机App最近更新了一个有趣的功能：用生成式AI来评论你的照片。根据The Verge和TechCrunch的报道，Indigo App的定位是“自然外观”的相机，但新功能让它变成了一个AI摄影教练。你可以拍一张照片，然后AI会给出构图、曝光、色彩等方面的建议，甚至可以直接用生成式AI帮你调整。这其实是Adobe在移动端AI摄影的一次重要尝试。Indigo最早是一个强调真实感的相机应用，但现在它拥抱了生成式AI，这反映了整个行业从“记录现实”到“增强现实”的转变。不过，这种“AI批评”功能是否会让摄影失去原本的随机乐趣？值得思考。

blocks.speaker: ian
blocks.text: 最后来看一个基础设施层面的新闻。在2026年世界人工智能大会上，上海仪电智算牵头成立了“智算系统架构联盟”，并发布了《超节点系统架构规范》。根据量子位的报道，这个联盟联合了23家产业链企业，包括GPU芯片公司（天数智芯、壁仞科技、平头哥、寒武纪等）、整机厂商（中兴、新华三、浪潮）、大模型公司（阶跃星辰、稀宇科技）以及软件公司。目标是解决算力供需不平衡和软硬件适配壁垒。简单说，就是让不同厂商的芯片、服务器、模型能协同工作，而不是各自为战。这个联盟由上海市经信委指导，仪电—中兴联合实验室负责运营，带有很强的政策推动色彩。对于国产算力生态来说，这是一个从“单点突破”转向“系统协作”的标志性事件。

blocks.speaker: ian
blocks.text: 回顾今天五个事件，你会发现一个共同的主题：标准化与边界之争。Sony起诉Udio，是在为AI时代的版权划定法律边界；Gritt用AI机器人建太阳能电站，是在为机器人施工建立效率标准；SpaceX进指数，是在为太空经济融入主流金融体系铺路；Adobe的AI相机，是在重新定义摄影的创作边界；而上海的智算架构联盟，则是在为国产算力建立技术标准。这些事件看似分散，但背后都是同一个问题：当新技术颠覆旧秩序时，谁有权制定规则？是法律、市场、还是技术本身？而作为观察者，我们需要关注的不仅是技术有多酷，更是这些规则将如何影响我们每个人的生活。

blocks.speaker: ian
blocks.text: 好了，今天的节目就到这里。如果你对AI版权、机器人建造或者指数基金投资有什么想法，欢迎在评论区留言。我是伊恩，我们明天见。
